title: blockdata
text: blockdata Defines a constant block of text. Explain this code BlockName: blockdata Delimiter ... ... text ... ... Delimiter Copy Example: TextA:blockdata + This is a block of text that can be assigned to a field or variable + Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description BlockName String Name of block; maximum of 8 characters. Delimiter String Beginning and end of the text block. text String All lines are considered part of the text until the character Delimiter is encountered. Return Values None Use Allowed in all component types. Description The blockdata statement is often used to define long sql statements. The following rules apply to the use of blockdata : The blockdata statement must be in the same ProcScript module as the statement that references the BlockName . The blockdata statement must be at the end of the ProcScript module. More than one blockdata statement is allowed in the ProcScript module. In a ProcScript statement (for example, assignment or putmess ), refer to BlockName as $ BlockName . BlockName cannot exceed eight characters in length. The hash character (#) is not allowed as Delimiter . Substituting variables (using two percent signs ( %% )) is not possible within text , although you can use $string to address this. For example: vBlock = $my_block ; does not substitute variables vBlockFinal = $string(vBlock) ; variables substituted Copy However, if the text contains compile-time constants such as <this_app_version> , these are not resolved. Example: Using blockdata The following example shows the use of the blockdata statement to define the text of a letter: ; trigger: Detail TEXT = $reject message "Standard refusal loaded in TEXT field." reject:blockdata + We regret to inform you that your qualifications do not match our current vacancy and that we have hired somebody else. Your application has been filed for future reference. Thank you for considering us. Yours sincerely,+ Copy The following example shows how to use blockdata in operations. The BlockData definition must be in the same ProcScript module in which it is referenced. operation GET_TEXT1 message/info $Text1 Text1:blockdata + This is Text One+ ; end operation GET_TEXT2 message/info $Text2 ; Text2:blockdata + This is text Two+ ; end Copy Related Links Script Modules A script module is a named collection of ProcScript belonging to a development object. Script modules are analogous to events and methods in other programming languages. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
